source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Pipeline Pump Fire at Ship Shoal Block 80

event.top_event: Flash fire during pipeline pump startup
actions_taken: Remaining flame extinguished using wheeled fire extinguisher unit
Two injured personnel evacuated via helicopter to Lady of the Sea Hospital
Injured personnel transported to University Hospital Burn Unit
BSEE Incident Follow-Up Inspection conducted on March 10, 2025
W&T conducted internal investigation and identified corrective actions
event.summary: On March 7, 2025, at 08:15 hours, a flash fire occurred at W&T Offshore's production facility at Ship Shoal Block 80, Platform A while attempting to bring Pipeline Pump #2 (PAX-360) online. A W&T Mechanic and Contractor Electrician sustained burn injuries to head and hands. The fire resulted from a loose fitting on the fuel gas starter exhaust piping that allowed gas to accumulate near the pump. When the engine backfired during startup, the accumulated gas ignited. Both injured personnel were evacuated and treated; the Electrician was released to return to work on April 9, 2025, while the Mechanic continued treatment. The incident was exacerbated by personnel not wearing required PPE (gloves) and wind direction that pushed flames toward one worker.
recommendations: When using fuel gas to start prime movers, always ensure the starter exhaust gas is vented to a safe location
Review gas engine startup procedures to include equipment checks verifying proper installation of components
Utilize gas detectors when performing startup procedures on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters
Conduct mandatory checks on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters to ensure proper installation of starter exhaust piping
Replace all flexible hoses with hard piping for fuel gas systems
Conduct routine checks on all fittings associated with starter gas
Stress the importance of wearing all required PPE during equipment startup operations

hazards.name: Natural gas engine with pneumatic starter

threats.name: Loose fitting on fuel gas exhaust piping
Fuel gas leakage and accumulation near ignition source

consequences.name: Thermal burns to personnel (head and hands)
Facility shutdown and loss of production

controls.name: Equipment maintenance - periodic tightening of fittings
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fuel gas leakage from loose connections
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent gas release

supporting_text: BSEE Investigators then identified an extremely loose connection/fitting on the vent exhaust piping coming from the Pipeline Pump #2's fuel gas starter
The incident would have been prevented had the equipment been maintained by periodically tightening fittings
Routine checks on all fittings associated with starter gas would have prevented the incident

controls.name: Pre-startup equipment inspection procedure
controls.barrier_role: Identify loose fittings, cracks, or corrosion before startup
controls.lod_basis: Procedural check to detect equipment defects

supporting_text: W&T described the procedure/process of the manual start of Pipeline Pump #2 as 'Visual inspection of unit, check oil, water, and fuel pressure, hit start'

when trouble shooting and/or attempting to start up natural gas-operated equipment, and particularly on equipment that has been down for an extended period of time, it is a best practice to check all hoses and fittings for cracks, corrosion, or loose fittings
review gas engine startup procedures to include equipment checks verifying proper installation of components

controls.name: Fuel gas exhaust venting to safe location
controls.barrier_role: Prevent accumulation of fuel gas near personnel and ignition sources
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to disperse hazardous gas

supporting_text: both the fuel gas inlet and vent exhaust piping consisted of flexible hoses
W&T management commented to the BSEE Investigators that they would be making recommendations to route all fuel gas through rigid steel pipe instead of a flexible hose
when using fuel gas to start prime movers, always ensure the starter exhaust gas is vented to a safe location

controls.name: Use of rigid steel piping instead of flexible hoses
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fuel gas leakage from hose degradation or loose connections
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design to improve system integrity

supporting_text: both the fuel gas inlet and vent exhaust piping consisted of flexible hoses
they will conduct blitzes/audits on gas-operated equipment on all W&T facilities to verify that all flexible hoses are replaced with hard piping

controls.name: Gas detection during startup procedures
controls.barrier_role: Detect accumulation of flammable gas before ignition
controls.lod_basis: Detection barrier to identify hazardous conditions

supporting_text: utilize gas detectors when performing startup procedures on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (gloves)
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of thermal burns to hands and arms
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: W&T verified that the IP's were not following proper PPE guidelines at the time of the incident, and that neither of the IP's were wearing gloves
The burns also would have been less severe or possibly prevented had the IP's worn the proper gloves for the job being performed
if the IPs were wearing the proper PPE at the time, they may have suffered less significant injuries

controls.name: Supervision and enforcement of PPE requirements
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel comply with required PPE during hazardous operations
controls.lod_basis: Management control to enforce safety practices

supporting_text: Not ensuring the Proper Use of PPE
stress the importance of wearing all required PPE moving forward
The facility was immediately shut-in and the remaining flame was extinguished
he then grabbed the nearest fire extinguisher, a rolling wheel unit, and extinguished the remaining flames
The two then grabbed a wheeled fire extinguisher unit and put out the remaining flame on the starter

controls.name: Emergency evacuation and medical response
controls.barrier_role: Evacuate injured personnel and provide emergency medical treatment
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize injury consequences

supporting_text: Both individuals were flown in by helicopter, initially seen at a hospital emergency room, then transported to the burn center
Both individuals were evacuated via crew change helicopter from the facility to Lady of the Sea Hospital, then transported to University Hospital Burn Unit for further treatment
He immediately initiated radio communication with the nearest manned facility Ship Shoal 28 to arrange a medivac for the IP's to be transported to the onshore
